name: Wilhelmina Rise
desc: Hillside above Kaimuki with sweeping views of Diamond Head and the coast — quiet residential streets, strong local feel.
insider: The panoramic views from upper Wilhelmina Rise rival expensive ridge neighborhoods at a fraction of the price — Diamond Head framed perfectly
It's essentially elevated Kaimuki — all the restaurants and shops on Waialae Ave are a quick walk (or steep walk back up)
Morning joggers love the steep streets — the grade on Wilhelmina Rise is a built-in cardio workout with ocean views
Homes rarely come on the market and sell fast when they do — the neighborhood has a fiercely loyal following
The higher lots along Sierra Drive and Lurline Drive have some of the best value-to-view ratios in all of Honolulu
Wilhelmina Rise Community Park has a small playground and green space — a quiet neighborhood gathering spot
The streets are steep enough that you should test your car's brakes before moving here — not an exaggeration
Kaimuki's restaurant scene (Koko Head Cafe, Mud Hen Water, 12th Ave Grill) is literally at the bottom of the hill — 5-minute drive
The neighborhood was developed in the 1940s-50s — homes have that charming mid-century Honolulu character with post-and-pier construction
Views of Koko Head, Diamond Head, and the ocean from the upper streets light up spectacularly at sunrise
